title: Still Standing: How European Firms Weathered the Crisis
summary_en: European firms, including in Hungary, weathered the 2008–9 crisis better than the macro data alone would suggest. The report uses the EFIGE survey (~14,000 firms across seven countries) to show that exporters and multi-product firms were systematically more resilient. Policy lessons focus on firm-level access to international markets and to credit during downturns.
policy_relevance: Direct input for Hungarian export-promotion (HIPA) and SME-finance design after the 2008 crisis. Argues that firm-level internationalisation is itself a stabiliser, so the policy levers worth using are export-credit access, trade finance, and FDI promotion — not blanket bailouts. Caveats: the survey is one cross-section; export-resilience may not generalise to demand-driven crises.
